signals.title: FTC Policy Statement on Age Verification Technologies
signals.summary: The FTC issued a policy statement indicating it will not enforce actions under COPPA against operators using personal information solely for age verification, provided they meet specific conditions.
key_provisions: Use collected information exclusively for age verification
Do not retain information longer than necessary
Provide clear notices to parents and children

signals.title: H.R. 10364 – [Title Not Specified]
signals.summary: A bill introduced to define 'verifiable parental consent' and outline requirements for obtaining such consent, including conditions for collecting and using age verification data.
key_provisions: Specifies conditions for age verification data collection and use

signals.title: Parents Over Platforms Act (Discussion Draft)
signals.summary: A draft bill prohibiting personalized advertising to minors and requiring developers to implement age verification mechanisms.
key_provisions: Mandates non-sharing of age verification data with third parties

signals.title: S. 2714 – [Title Not Specified]
signals.summary: A bill requiring entities to verify users' ages using commercially available methods and mandating parental consent for minors accessing certain online services.
key_provisions: Requires age verification using commercially available methods
Prohibits minors' access to AI chatbots with explicit content

signals.title: FTC Workshop on Age Verification Technologies
signals.summary: The FTC hosted a virtual workshop to discuss age verification technologies and their role in complying with COPPA.
key_provisions: Discussed effectiveness and challenges of age verification methods
Explored balance between user privacy and child protection
